QF.id: VIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_15

question.text: Price discovery in futures markets is considered superior to forward markets because:

answer.text: Futures prices are determined by a large number of buyers and sellers on a transparent electronic order book, reflecting broad market consensus
SEBI fixes the futures prices every morning before trading begins, ensuring fair and accurate pricing
Futures prices are always exactly equal to the theoretical fair value calculated using the cost of carry model
Only expert traders are allowed to trade in futures, so the prices are more informed and accurate

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Price discovery in futures is superior because: (1) large number of diverse participants (hedgers, speculators, arbitrageurs), (2) centralized electronic order book visible to all, (3) real-time price updates, and (4) competitive bidding ensures prices reflect all available information. In forwards, prices are negotiated bilaterally and not publicly available.
**The Trap:** SEBI does not fix prices. Actual futures prices may deviate from theoretical fair value. Retail investors also trade futures, not just experts.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Price discovery function of derivatives is tested in Series VIII Chapter 2 and 3.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Transparent + centralized + many participants = efficient price discovery in futures markets.
